attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 DCD (Disabilities & Congenital Disorders) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. This is NET-NEW coverage (the 7th tranche-1 category, not a base-303 refresh) — ingest grows the eval corpus 303->353. Bidirectional tier calibration reviewed and accepted: the routine longitudinal management of a stable congenital/genetic/developmental disability is low-tier (self-care + specialist/allied-health/MDT referral + safety-net), while acute decompensations and time-critical complications are tiered to true acuity (T3-T5) with mandatory escalation edges — autonomic dysreflexia in spinal cord injury (T5/000), metabolic crises (MSUD/galactosaemia/mitochondrial/peroxisomal/hypoglycaemia), aspiration pneumonia, progressive neuromuscular respiratory failure, major burns, pressure-injury sepsis, status epilepticus, decompensated congenital heart disease, hypocalcaemic tetany, and demand myocardial ischaemia in supravalvular aortic stenosis. Paediatric no-dose hard limit honoured throughout (no synthesised mg/kg or weight-based dose; paediatric and unknown-weight cases flagged for in-person/specialist prescribing), no autonomous diagnosis, diagnostic-overshadowing encoded as the key error trap. Specific borderlines reviewed and accepted: (a) DCD-016 source file titled 'Perthes' but the clinical picture is developmental dysplasia of the hip in a 4-month-old — authored correctly as DDH, source title is a note-quirk only; (b) the T5-heavy tier profile (T3x9/T4x10/T5x31) is accepted as honest for a folder authored entirely as acute-complication presentations; (c) DCD-050 Williams syndrome panic attack tiered T5 is accepted (fixed supravalvular aortic stenosis + tachycardia -> demand myocardial ischaemia, the note's own primary differential is acute MI).
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all DCD casebundles in this batch (n=50)

codes_manifest.display: Pneumonitis due to food and vomit (aspiration pneumonia)
Hepatomegaly with splenomegaly, not elsewhere classified
Acute respiratory failure, unspecified whether with hypoxia or hypercapnia

transform_flags: Provisional case_id SEQ (SPEC-GENET-06-00229) derived from source DCD-029 — maintainer to confirm/reassign within the SPEC-GENET-06 bucket at ingest --reseq.

Specialty_tags set to [PAEDS,RESP,ENDO] (the 00-schema enum has no GENET/PALL code; ENDO=endocrinology & metabolism is the closest home for a lysosomal storage disease). The case_id keeps the descriptive GENET partition-key (id is an opaque name per id_rule; metadata is authoritative) — reviewer may re-prioritise tags at no cost to the seal.
Age (6) and sex (male) taken from source ('6-year-old child', 'his'); weight 18 kg noted. Please confirm on clinician review.
PAEDIATRIC NO-DOSE: patient ~18 kg — no weight-based antibiotic or drug dose synthesised; parenteral antibiotic (ceftriaxone) dosing flagged for the receiving hospital/paramedic team (hard limit).

Telehealth reprojection applied: in-person clinician actions (auscultation, palpation of hepatosplenomegaly, deep Yankauer oropharyngeal suctioning, high-flow non-rebreather oxygen, stat IM ceftriaxone, measured BP 85/50) reprojected to the AI-appropriate answer — recognise the emergency, call 000, and coach the carer through home suction/oxygen/positioning. Clinician-only exam/measurements sealed into 10/11 with telehealth_limits_for_this_case.

Presentation layer de-anchored: the KNOWN baseline lysosomal storage disease + kyphoscoliosis + hepatosplenomegaly + epilepsy are carried in 01 as carer-stated history (legitimate); the NEW acute diagnosis (aspiration pneumonia with respiratory failure) and the correct T5 triage are sealed in 10-13.

SpO2 82%, HR ~165 and Temp 38.6 reprojected as caregiver/home-device objective_data_offered (verified:false) consistent with a palliative home setup that has an oximeter and thermometer; measured BP and auscultation kept sealed as clinician-only.

Palliative / advance-care-directive tension encoded in clinical_uncertainty_handling and ESC-003: the source note's own disposition was emergency escalation (000 to PICU), so baseline tier is T5; the AI must not unilaterally withhold escalation on palliative grounds.
Candidate codes only — every SNOMED/ICD-10-AM/AMT code is unverified_pending_terminology_receipt; the AMT code for ceftriaxone is a SNOMED-substance candidate pending terminology confirmation.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
